additional_findings.title: isPreflopRoundComplete ignores folded players when checking actedAfter
additional_findings.detail:
The function tracks which positions have called/folded after the last raise via `actedAfter.set.add(position)`, but players who folded BEFORE the last raise never appear in this set. The final loop checks ALL 5 other positions after the raiser, including already-folded players. This causes a false negative — the function returns false for genuinely complete rounds.

Example: [{UTG:fold}, {HJ:fold}, {CO:raise}, {BTN:call}, {SB:call}, {BB:call}]
- lastRaiserIdx=2 (CO), actedAfter={BTN, SB, BB}
- Loop: BTN✓ SB✓ BB✓ UTG✗(folded before raise, never in actedAfter) → returns false
- Reality: Only CO,BTN,SB,BB are active — all have acted. Round IS complete.

Impact: Auto-transition from preflop→postflop silently fails. User gets stuck in preflop mode after any scenario where a player folded before the last raise.
additional_findings.fix: Track active (non-folded) positions and only check those against actedAfter. Or filter actedAfter to only include non-folded positions before the last-raise check.

additional_findings.title: advanceToNextStreet does not clear auto-advance timer
additional_findings.detail:
advanceToNextStreet() (line 544) modifies state but never calls `clearTimeout(autoAdvanceRef.current)`. If a user:
1. Takes an action → 1.5s auto-advance timer starts
2. Manually clicks "Advance to Turn" before timer fires
3. Timer fires 1.5s from step 1 → calls advanceToNextStreetRef.current() again
4. Double-advances to river with a default 'check' action for the skipped turn street

The Continue to Next Street button (line 1008) calls advanceToNextStreet directly with no timer guard. Additionally, config panel inputs for board/pot/stack (lines 649-657) reset street navigation but also don't clear the timer.
additional_findings.fix: Add `if (autoAdvanceRef.current) clearTimeout(autoAdvanceRef.current)` at the start of advanceToNextStreet(). Also add to config panel change handlers.

additional_findings.title: Hardcoded production URL in CI workflow
additional_findings.detail: BASE_URL is hardcoded to 'https://wiz.codeovertcp.com' instead of using a GitHub Actions secret or environment variable. This URL is specific to one deployment and will break if the deployment URL changes or in forked repos.
additional_findings.fix: Use ${{ secrets.BASE_URL }} or ${{ vars.BASE_URL }} with a repository variable.

additional_findings.title: || instead of ?? for pot_size fallback
additional_findings.detail: `setPfPot(treeNode?.pot_size || 5.5)` — the `||` operator will default to 5.5 if pot_size is literally 0 (edge case). Should use `??` (nullish coalescing) to only fallback when pot_size is null/undefined.

additional_findings.title: Position order hardcoded to 6-max
additional_findings.detail: POSITION_ORDER is hardcoded as ['UTG','HJ','CO','BTN','SB','BB']. Both isPreflopRoundComplete and isAllFold depend on exactly 6 entries with wrap-around modulo arithmetic. Heads-up (2 players) or short-handed (3-5) configurations would produce incorrect results via wrong index math.
additional_findings.fix: Derive POSITION_ORDER from game configuration or make it a parameter.

conflicts.item: CI workflow references non-existent E2E test file
conflicts.my_assessment: CONFIRMED — e2e/study-game-tree.spec.ts is absent (verified by ls e2e/). This would cause CI failure on any triggering push/PR. Primary verdict: APPROVE despite this; I agree it's a minor issue that doesn't block approval.
